LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSES THAT THE GENERAL COUNSEL OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA IS A CLOSE FRIEND OF HIS, BUT NEITHER THAT FRIENDSHIP NOR ANY OTHER MATTER CAUSES HIM TO BE OTHER THAN FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN DECIDING THIS CASE.DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION- PLAINTIFF'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION IN VIOLATION OF THE FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING ACT (FEHA) IS CONTINUED FOR A HEARING ON JUNE 25, 2018. PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF TO BE FILED BY JUNE 13 2018. SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION TO BE FILED BY JUNE 20, 2018. PLAINTIFF'S SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AGAINST DEFENDANT DR. BASBAUM IS GRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF CANNOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THAT DEFENDANT BAUSBAUM ENGAGED IN INTENTIONAL OR OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT THAT PLAINTIFF SUFFERED "SEVERE" EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600 =(302/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Louis Silver, Esq. 408-456-5027 of S & K for Plaintiff Nripendra Dhillo, who is also present.
Marie Trimble Holvick, Esq. 415-875-4242 of Gordon & Rees for Defendant REGENTS OF THE UNVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA.

tra Plaintif PPO

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants The Regents of the University of California and Alan Busbaum's motion for summary judgment on the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Nripendra Dhillon is denied as to the first cause of action for retaliation which is only alleged against the Regents and is granted as to the second cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress which is only alleged against Dr. Basbaum. Liberally construing the evidence presented by Dr. Dhillon, that evidence creates a triable dispute whether Dr. Dhillon's protected conduct of complaining that he was discriminated against was a substantial factor in the reduction of Dr. Dhillon's appointment percentage, salary and benefits for the 2016-17 academic year. The undisputed facts establish that Dr. Busbaum did not commit any extreme or outrageous conduct and Dr. Dhillon did suffer severe emotional distress.

Counsel for Regents to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and CCP 437c(g).

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Continued To Jun-25-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Louis D. Silver of S & K Law Firm, tel. No. 1-408-456-5027 appearing
for the Plaintiff;
Nripendra Dhion, Plaintiff also present in court;
Atty. Marie Trimble Holvick & Atty. Amber A. Eklof of Gordon & Rees, Tel.
No. 1-415-875-4242 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - Judge Kahn discloses that the General Counsel of the University of California is a close friend of his, but neither that friendship nor any other matter causes him to be other than fair and impartial in deciding this case. The matter was argued. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication- Plaintiff's First Cause of Action for retaliation in violation of the Fair Employment and Housing Act (FEHA) is continued for a hearing on June 25, 2018. Plaintiff's Supplemental Brief to be filed by June 13 2018. Supplemental Opposition to be filed by June 20, 2018. Plaintiff's Second Cause of Action for intentional infliction of emotional distress against Defendant Dr. Basbaum is granted because Plaintiff cannot create a triable issue of fact that Defendant Bausbaum engaged in intentional or outrageous conduct that Plaintiff suffered "severe" emotional distress. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =(302/HEK)
